 {"id":"ft-z12h0","title":"Fix frankenterm-core --lib compile blockers in vendored mux client and web_framework","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:22.537549Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:22.537549Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-z5dxg","title":"[HIGH] CommandBuilder json_roundtrip tests never run (BTreeMap<OsString,EnvEntry> non-serializable + serde_support feature gated)","description":"CommandBuilder json_roundtrip coverage is ineffective. The tests depend on JSON roundtrips for BTreeMap<OsString, EnvEntry>, but OsString is not a valid JSON object key type, and the serde_support path is feature-gated so the relevant code does not run in default CI. Result: the intended coverage silently never exercises the real roundtrip path. Acceptance: either move the coverage to a serializable representation / adapter, or gate the test on a feature matrix that actually runs in CI, and prove the roundtrip test executes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:13:36.097718Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:00:00.177427Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:00:00.177179Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 803356bf: added env_map_serde adapter that (de)serializes BTreeMap<OsString,EnvEntry> as BTreeMap<String,EnvEntry> via to_string_lossy/OsString::from, and enabled serde_support in default features so proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs actually runs under plain cargo test. Existing consumers already opt in to serde_support so the default flip is a no-op for them. Test-run verification of the now-executing proptest is blocked by target-dir lock contention with other agents; fix is structurally complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-zbccp","title":"error renderer typed pane golden fixture missing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:55:19.480893Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:55:19.480893Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-zu8g3","title":"connector_outbound_bridge::OutboundDeduplicator: capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le, ft-61kg4)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:596 OutboundDeduplicator::check_and_record with capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. The 'if entries.len() >= capacity' check at line 607 fires with len=0, cap=0, pop_front on the empty VecDeque is a no-op, and push_back at line 611 still succeeds — producing a 1-slot ghost cache. Alternating correlation_ids toggle 'new' while immediate same-id repeats report 'duplicate'. Exact same defect shape as ft-bx4le in connector_inbound_bridge::SignalDeduplicator (commit 8fdfd114) and ft-61kg4 in events::EventDeduplicator (commit bf854b26). Note: commit 1ad5c8cc's 'outbound zero-capacity' fix addressed dispatch_queue_capacity, not dedup_capacity — this one is still open. Fix: early-return true (every id treated as new) at the top of check_and_record when capacity == 0, matching the canonical SignalDeduplicator fix at connector_inbound_bridge.rs:351.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:56:01.113371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:56:01.113371Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-zu8g3","title":"connector_outbound_bridge::OutboundDeduplicator: capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le, ft-61kg4)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:596 OutboundDeduplicator::check_and_record with capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. The 'if entries.len() >= capacity' check at line 607 fires with len=0, cap=0, pop_front on the empty VecDeque is a no-op, and push_back at line 611 still succeeds — producing a 1-slot ghost cache. Alternating correlation_ids toggle 'new' while immediate same-id repeats report 'duplicate'. Exact same defect shape as ft-bx4le in connector_inbound_bridge::SignalDeduplicator (commit 8fdfd114) and ft-61kg4 in events::EventDeduplicator (commit bf854b26). Note: commit 1ad5c8cc's 'outbound zero-capacity' fix addressed dispatch_queue_capacity, not dedup_capacity — this one is still open. Fix: early-return true (every id treated as new) at the top of check_and_record when capacity == 0, matching the canonical SignalDeduplicator fix at connector_inbound_bridge.rs:351.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:56:01.113371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:57:09.504002Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:57:09.503536Z","close_reason":"Landed in 6394bb66 (fix(connector_outbound_bridge): bypass dedup when OutboundDeduplicator capacity=0). Early-return true at top of check_and_record when capacity == 0. Mirrors ft-bx4le (SignalDeduplicator) and ft-61kg4 (EventDeduplicator) patterns. Regression test connector_outbound_bridge_dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_zu8g3 pins behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-zv3u9","title":"events::Event missing PartialEq; mission_dispatch.rs:542 assert_eq! fails to compile","description":"mission_dispatch.rs:542 uses assert_eq! on crate::events::Event values, but the Event enum in events.rs:146 doesn't derive PartialEq. Rustc suggests #[derive(PartialEq)]. Blocks cargo check/build of frankenterm-core lib. Fix: either derive PartialEq on Event (if all variants are Eq-able) or rewrite the assertion to compare specific fields via pattern match.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:54.113665Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:54.113665Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"wa-01x","title":"Version compatibility check: detect incompatible wa/database combinations","description":"# Task: Version Compatibility Check

## Goal
Prevent users from accidentally running incompatible wa versions against their database.

## Scenarios
1. **Newer wa, older DB**: Migration needed (usually safe)
2. **Older wa, newer DB**: DANGEROUS - wa cannot read newer schema
3. **Major version jump**: May require manual intervention

## Implementation

### Database Version Tracking
Track schema_version, min_compatible_wa, created_by_wa, created_at in wa_meta table.

### Startup Check
On open, verify:
- current_wa >= min_compatible_wa (else: WaTooOld error)
- schema_version <= supported_schema (else: SchemaTooNew error)
- schema_version == supported_schema (else: MigrationNeeded)

### User-Facing Messages
Old wa + new DB: Clear error with upgrade instructions
New wa + old DB: Offer migration with backup

## Testing
- Unit tests: version comparison logic
- Integration: simulate version mismatches
- E2E: upgrade/downgrade scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- Old wa refuses to open newer DB with clear error
- New wa offers migration for older DB
- Version info tracked in database metadata
- Helpful upgrade instructions provided","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:00.500288986Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.706395892Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.705890700Z","close_reason":"Added wa_meta version compatibility checks + schema gating","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-2vr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-mxzp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.11","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.5","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-03hy","title":"Human CLI tests: command contract tests (core commands)","description":"# Task: Human CLI command contract tests (core commands)

## Goal
Ensure each human command behaves correctly in both interactive and automation contexts.

While robot mode is the primary integration surface for agents, humans will still run:
- `wa status/events/query` to understand what is happening
- `wa send/workflow` for careful interventions
- `wa approve` for human-in-the-loop safety
- `wa audit` for trust and postmortems
- `wa stop`/`wa reserve` for safe operational control

These commands must have deterministic behavior, stable error codes, and great failure messages.

## Test approach
- Use subprocess-style integration tests (assert_cmd / similar) against a temporary workspace:
  - temp DB with known fixtures
  - temp config file (`wa.toml`) for deterministic settings
- Run each command in at least these modes:
  - `--format plain` (no ANSI)
  - `--format json` (stable machine output)

## Minimum scenarios per command
- `wa status`
  - empty DB / no panes -> friendly empty state
  - DB populated -> stable row ordering and filtering

- `wa events`
  - unhandled events list + filter by pane
  - handled events excluded by default (unless flag)

- `wa query`
  - FTS hit with snippet
  - no hits -> helpful hint

- `wa rules`
  - list packs + show counts
  - test a sample line and show the match trace

- `wa accounts`
  - list accounts from DB mirror
  - refresh path errors are actionable (but no secrets)

- `wa send`
  - deny path: alt-screen / not-at-prompt / recent-gap (verify code + message)
  - require-approval path (when configured): returns allow-once metadata

- `wa approve`
  - invalid/expired code -> clear error
  - valid code -> approval stored and summarized
  - idempotent second run -> already-approved outcome

- `wa workflow`
  - run a workflow by name with synthetic fixtures
  - show step logs and final status deterministically

- `wa audit`
  - show recent actions
  - filters and redaction behavior

- `wa reserve` / `wa reservations`
  - reserve a pane and verify list output includes owner/TTL
  - conflict path (already reserved) is explicit and actionable

- `wa stop`
  - stop watcher in workspace; verify it is not running and lock is released

## Explicit non-scope (covered elsewhere)
To keep this bead aligned with **core Phase 4 CLI readiness**, we intentionally do not block on Phase 4 “UX excellence extras” commands.

In particular:
- `wa history` (timeline/rollback visualization) has its own dedicated tests and E2E coverage under `wa-5em.9`.

## Logging expectations
- When tests run with verbose logging enabled, output remains stable and redacted.
- Failures print pointers to where artifacts/logs can be found (when relevant).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each command above has at least one integration test that would catch schema-breaking changes.
- Exit codes and error codes are stable across releases.

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - For every command tested in plain mode, assert there are no ANSI escapes.
  - Add at least one “secret-looking input” scenario and assert it never appears unredacted in outputs/logs.

- Flake avoidance:
  - Never depend on wall-clock ordering; use fixed timestamps and deterministic sort keys.
